themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing parties in Spain, Italy, and France are advancing policies that prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to housing, healthcare, and public services. This theme reflects a coordinated rhetorical and legislative effort to institutionalize nationalist preferences, often framed as protecting cultural identity and social cohesion. The policies are sparking debates over legality, human rights, and the role of the state in mediating access to essential services.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Proposes 'national priority' in housing and healthcare, sparking clashes with regional governments and opposition parties.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Advocates for policies favoring Italian citizens in public services, aligning with Vox's rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Promotes similar nationalist policies, emphasizing French citizens' priority in welfare and employment.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Proposes prioritizing locals for social housing and scrapping climate measures, echoing continental far-right policies.

themes.title: AfD’s surge dominates German far-right coverage
themes.blurb: Germany's Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) is leading in polls across multiple states, particularly in Saxony-Anhalt, where it is poised to secure an absolute majority. This surge reflects broader discontent with the ruling coalition and has sparked debates over the party's democratic legitimacy, its ties to extremism, and the potential for a far-right government in Germany. The AfD's rise is also fueling internal conflicts within other parties and prompting calls for bans or stricter regulations.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Leads polls in Saxony-Anhalt with 41%, sparking debates over its democratic legitimacy and potential to form a government.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Monitors AfD's rise as a potential ally or competitor in European far-right politics.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Watches AfD's trajectory closely, with some factions expressing concern over its extremist ties.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Covers AfD's rise as part of broader European far-right trends, drawing parallels to its own policies.

themes.title: Corruption scandals rock far-right governments
themes.blurb: Corruption allegations are undermining far-right governments and parties in Argentina, Hungary, and Spain. In Argentina, the scandal surrounding Manuel Adorni's alleged illicit enrichment has led to internal divisions within the Milei administration. In Hungary, the Tisza Party faces scrutiny over opaque campaign financing and nepotism. Meanwhile, Spain's Vox is grappling with internal resignations and financial controversies. These scandals are eroding public trust and fueling debates over transparency and accountability in far-right governance.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: Manuel Adorni faces allegations of illicit enrichment, sparking internal divisions and public backlash.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Tisza Party faces scrutiny over opaque campaign financing and nepotism in ministerial appointments.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Internal resignations and financial controversies undermine party credibility ahead of elections.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Faces corruption allegations involving Zondacrypto and ties to Russian mafia, damaging its reputation.

themes.title: Trump’s Iran war dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The ongoing conflict between the U.S. and Iran under President Trump's administration is a dominant theme in right-wing media across the U.S., Italy, Poland, and the UK. The war has sparked debates over U.S. foreign policy, the role of NATO allies, and the potential for a diplomatic resolution. Right-wing parties are reacting to the conflict with a mix of support for Trump's hardline stance and criticism of his handling of the situation, particularly regarding the Strait of Hormuz and the impact on global energy markets.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's Iran policy dominates U.S. right-wing media, with debates over military strategy and diplomatic efforts.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Covers the Iran war as part of broader discussions on U.S.-Italy relations and NATO commitments.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Analyzes the Iran conflict through the lens of U.S.-Poland relations and NATO security.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Critiques Trump's Iran policy, focusing on its impact on global stability and UK interests.

themes.title: Far-right surges in European polls and elections
themes.blurb: Far-right parties are making significant gains in polls and elections across Europe, including Germany, Sweden, the UK, Norway, and France. This surge reflects growing public dissatisfaction with mainstream parties, particularly on issues like immigration, economic policy, and national sovereignty. The far-right's rise is reshaping political landscapes, forcing mainstream parties to adopt more nationalist rhetoric and policies to counter the appeal of far-right alternatives.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Leads polls in multiple German states, including Saxony-Anhalt, with 41% support.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Gains traction in Swedish polls, challenging mainstream parties on immigration and welfare policies.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Projected to make significant gains in UK local elections, siphoning support from Labour and Conservatives.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Increases influence in Norwegian politics, particularly on immigration and economic issues.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Leads French polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election, shaping national debates on immigration and security.
